docs.title: BERT Document Classifier
docs.text: A high-performance NLP pipeline for automated document classification using the BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) architecture.

docs.title: 🎯 Overview
docs.text: This project aims to provide a robust, production-ready solution for categorizing documents with high accuracy. Originally developed for the Smart India Hackathon 2023, this implementation focuses on optimized preprocessing and scalable deployment.

docs.title: 🚀 Key Features
docs.text:
• Context-Aware Classification: Leverages BERT's bidirectional understanding of language.
  • Optimized Pipeline: 40% faster preprocessing through batching and efficient tokenization.
  • API-First: Built with FastAPI for high-performance inference.
  • Containerized: Fully Dockerized for seamless deployment on AWS EC2 or other cloud providers.

docs.title: 🛠️ Tech Stack
docs.text:
• Language: Python 3.11+
  • Model: HuggingFace Transformers (BERT)
  • API: FastAPI
  • Inference: PyTorch
  • DevOps: Docker, AWS EC2

For detailed development instructions, see DEV_README.md.

docs.title: AI Agent Guidelines (AGENT.md)
docs.text: This file provides critical context and constraints for AI agents (like Gemini CLI) working on this project.

docs.title: 🤖 Role
docs.text: You are a Senior Machine Learning Engineer helping to maintain and evolve this BERT-based classifier.

docs.title: 📝 Standards & Practices
docs.text:
• Granular Commits: Every distinct development step (e.g., directory creation, dependency installation, single-feature addition) must be committed individually with descriptive messages. This ensures high traceability and easy backtracking.
  • Documentation: All code changes must be reflected in documentation. Every tool call that modifies logic must consider if DECISIONS.md or CHANGELOG.md needs an update.
  • Versioning: Follow Semantic Versioning 2.0.0.
  • Coding Style: PEP 8 for Python. Type hints are mandatory for all function signatures.
  • Testing: Every feature must have a corresponding test in the tests/ directory.

docs.title: 🎯 Current Objectives
docs.text:
1. Initialize project structure.
  2. Establish documentation baseline.
  3. Prepare for "Hello World" BERT implementation.

docs.title: 🚫 Constraints
docs.text:
• Do not introduce large dependencies without logging the decision in DECISIONS.md.
  • Ensure all API endpoints are documented via FastAPI's Swagger UI.

docs.title: Changelog (CHANGELOG.md)
docs.text: All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file. The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial project directory structure.
  • Comprehensive documentation suite (README, DEV_README, DECISIONS, AGENT, CHANGELOG, FINAL).
  • Project subdirectories (src, tests, data, models).
  • Python virtual environment and core dependencies (transformers, torch, fastapi, uvicorn).
  • Initial boilerplate in src/main.py with FastAPI and BERT tokenizer integration.

docs.title: Architectural Decisions (DECISIONS.md)
docs.text: All significant architectural and technical decisions are logged here.

docs.title: 2026-05-03: Initial Project Structure
docs.text:
• Decision: Adopt a multi-file documentation strategy (README, DEV_README, DECISIONS, AGENT, CHANGELOG, FINAL).
  • Reason: User preference for heavy documentation and clear separation of concerns between user-facing docs, developer docs, and AI context.
  • Status: Accepted.

docs.title: 2026-05-03: Base Model Selection
docs.text:
• Decision: Use bert-base-uncased from HuggingFace.
  • Reason: Industry standard for general-purpose document classification with a good balance between performance and resource consumption.
  • Status: Proposed.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 or higher
  • pip and venv
  • Docker (optional, for containerization)

docs.title: Local Development
docs.text:
1. Create Virtual Environment: 
    python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
  2. Install Dependencies: 
    pip install transformers torch fastapi uvicorn scikit-learn

docs.title: 🏗️ Project Structure
docs.text:
BERT-Document-Classifier/
├── src/                # Source code
├── data/               # Datasets (gitignored)
├── models/             # Local model checkpoints
├── tests/              # Unit and integration tests
├── AGENT.md            # AI Agent guidelines
├── DECISIONS.md        # Architectural decisions
└── CHANGELOG.md        # Semantic versioning log

docs.title: 🧪 Testing
docs.text:
Run tests using pytest: 

pytest

docs.title: 📦 Deployment
docs.text:
Build the Docker image: 

docker build -t bert-classifier .

docs.title: Project Vision & Final Goals (FINAL.md)
docs.text: This document defines the "Definition of Done" for the version of the project we want to achieve.

docs.title: 🏁 The Ultimate Goal
docs.text: A production-grade, highly optimized document classification service capable of handling diverse document types (PDF, Text, Images via OCR) with >90% accuracy and sub-200ms inference time.

docs.title: 🎯 Key Milestones for "Final"
docs.text:
• [ ] Model Excellence: Fine-tuned BERT or RoBERTa model achieving state-of-the-art performance on our target dataset.
  • [ ] Auto-Scaling Inference: Deployed on Kubernetes or AWS Lambda/ECS with auto-scaling based on request volume.
  • [ ] Observability: Complete monitoring dashboard (Grafana/Prometheus) tracking accuracy, latency, and drift.
  • [ ] Active Learning Loop: Mechanism to flag low-confidence predictions for human review and re-incorporate them into training.
  • [ ] Multi-Modal Support: Integrated OCR (Tesseract or AWS Textract) to handle scanned images and PDFs.

docs.title: 📈 Success Metrics
docs.text:
• Accuracy: >90% on hold-out test set.
  • Latency: P99 < 300ms.
  • Scalability: Handle 100+ concurrent requests.
